prerequisite: Characterization tests must be written before Phase 1. No extraction without a safety net.

scoring_dimensions.test_coverage: weight 20% — inverse: no tests = high extraction risk
scoring_dimensions.data_tangle: weight 25% — shared or entangled data model
scoring_dimensions.business_criticality: weight 25% — production impact if extraction goes wrong

ranked_seams.name: Operational Safety
ranked_seams.recommendation: Delete immediately — security risk. ErrorController exposes unauthenticated crash endpoints (System.exit, OOM loop). Actuator wildcard exposes env vars, credentials and heap dumps publicly. Must be removed before any deployment to any environment.
ranked_seams.rationale: This is not an extraction — it is a deletion. ErrorController has no business value and is a critical security liability. Actuator misconfiguration leaks credentials via /actuator/env. Fixing this in Phase 0 costs nothing and eliminates the highest-severity risks in the entire codebase.

ranked_seams.name: Album Catalog Service
ranked_seams.recommendation: Extract first — this is the only business capability. Group controller, domain entity, and all repository implementations together. Do not extract the web layer separately from the domain: a controller without its entity and repository is non-functional.
ranked_seams.rationale: AlbumController calls the repository interface directly with a clean CrudRepository boundary. The main complication is that Album carries JPA annotations but is reused for MongoDB — this dual-purpose POJO must be resolved during extraction. Target: Spring Boot 3.x, Java 17, PostgreSQL as default. The lack of test coverage is the biggest risk: characterization tests must be written first.
blockers: No characterization tests — write these before touching any code
Album entity has JPA annotations but is reused for MongoDB — resolve before extraction
PUT/POST semantics are inverted in AlbumController — fix during extraction, not before

ranked_seams.name: Data Initialization
ranked_seams.recommendation: Extract alongside Album Catalog Service, not before or after. If left in the monolith when S1 is extracted, the new service starts empty. If extracted before S1, it has nothing to seed. The two must travel together.
ranked_seams.rationale: AlbumRepositoryPopulator is a hidden business rule (seed on first startup if empty) buried in an ApplicationReadyEvent listener. The count()==0 guard must become explicit during extraction — migrate to a DataInitializer component or a Flyway seed migration inside the new catalog service.
blockers: Easy to forget — not visible from the API surface, no tests cover it
Must be made opt-in via Spring profile in production (sample data in a logistics catalog is unprofessional — see USER_STORIES.md CAP-4)

ranked_seams.name: Platform / Infrastructure Adapter
ranked_seams.recommendation: Do not extract — dissolve. SpringApplicationContextInitializer is tightly coupled to Cloud Foundry VCAP_SERVICES and runs before the Spring context is built, making it untestable. As CF dependency is removed and replaced with standard SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE env vars, this class becomes irrelevant and is deleted, not migrated.
ranked_seams.rationale: InfoController does new CfEnv() directly (cannot be injected or mocked). SpringApplicationContextInitializer runs at bootstrap time — any failure here prevents the app from starting. Hardcoded service-tag-to-profile mapping makes it brittle for any new environment. Short-term: inject CfEnv into InfoController. Medium-term: replace all CF detection with env-var config — the class dissolves naturally.
blockers: InfoController instantiates CfEnv directly — refactor to inject before extracting
SpringApplicationContextInitializer runs before beans are created — extremely hard to test
Hardcoded HashMap of CF service tags to profiles — brittle, must be replaced with env-var config

phase_0.label: Security First
actions: Restrict Actuator to health and info only
Write characterization tests against current API

phase_1.label: Extract Business Capability
actions: Extract Album Catalog Service (S1) + Data Initialization (S2) together
New service: Spring Boot 3.x, Java 17, PostgreSQL
API Facade routes /albums/* to new service
Monolith /albums kept alive until contract tests pass

phase_2.label: Remove CF Dependency
actions: Replace CF profile detection with SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE env var
